title: In vitro class switching staining for flow cytometry

Pharmingen)\r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"maboh","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"vevvp","text":"Biotinylated anti-IgG1, anti-IgG2a-2b (both Pharmingen)\r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ogxjt","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"qfuas","text":"Streptavidin conjugate (Pharmingen)\r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cxkga","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"issmh","text":"FACS buffer: PBS + 0.5% fetal calf serum + 5mM EDTA","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Harvest splenocytes by grinding of spleens in RPMI-1640 medium between two frosted microscope slides. Spin cells at 1000 RPM for 5 min. Remove erythrocytes by adding one equivalent volume of ACK Lysing Buffer to the remaining pellet. Gently resuspend, swirl tube for 1 min. Fill tube with 10 ml of RPMI-1640, centrifuge at 1000 RPM for 5 min. Resuspend in 10 ml of RPMI-1640. Filter through 70 μm nylon cell strainer. Centrifuge at 1000 RPM for 5 min. Resuspend 1 × 107 cells in 500 μl of FACS buffer. Obtain B cells by CD43 negative purification using anti-CD43 magnetic beads.

Dilute CD43- B cells to a concentration of 5×105 cells/ml in B cell complete medium. Use 24-well plates. Incubate 5×105 B cells in presence of 10 μg/ml of anti-CD40 (clone 3/23), plus 10 ng/ml of recombinant mouse IL-4 for IgG1 analysis (6). Incubate for 4 d in a humidified 37 o C, 5% CO2 incubator.

Wash cells with FACS buffer. Preincubate cells on ice for 10 min in presence of Fc blocker (anti-CD16/CD32). We usually use 0.5 μl of Fc blocker/ 5×105 cells in a total volume of 100 μl. CRITICAL STEP: Good Fc blockade.
Stain cells with biotinylated anti-IgG1, anti-IgG2a-2b (we used the antibodies at a dilution of 1:200 in a total volume of 100 μl). Incubate on ice for 20 min. Wash 2x with FACS buffer.
Stain cells with streptavidin conjugate of your choosing. Wash 2x with FACS buffer.

Kinase MEKK1 is required for CD40-dependent activation of the kinases Jnk and p38, germinal center formation, B cell proliferation and antibody production by Gallagher, E. et al. published in Nature Immunology on 22 Jan, 2007 https://dx.doi.org/10.1038/ni1421
creator: Protocols Importer
